hero.name: David Pilný
hero.bio: Builds interfaces in Vue and TypeScript. Co-founder of MIDAFIT, a fitness education platform. Reads 19th-century parish registers for fun.

colophon.title: Colophon
colophon.programmeIntro: The palette on this site is generated, not chosen. One base hue; a regular polygon on the OKLCH colour wheel; equal chroma at every vertex, so each slot carries the same perceived saturation. Karl Gerstner called this writing a programme: state the rule, then let the rule produce the outcome.
colophon.lightness: Two derived lightnesses. L 0.663 carries chips and swatches on chalk. L 0.520 carries any surface with chalk text on it — the highest lightness that holds 4.5:1 against chalk at every hue of the wheel, measured, so the slider below cannot break the contrast floor. Slot 3 runs its chroma at the sRGB ceiling (0.23): same geometry, louder. It marks status.
colophon.demo: Drag. Every colour on this page derives from this one number. The choice is stored in your browser and nowhere else.
colophon.legendNote: Colour never appears without meaning. Six hues meaning nothing is noise wearing a grid.
colophon.typeBody: Archivo, one variable file, width axis at 125% for display. JetBrains Mono for metadata. A 24px baseline; nothing below 11px.
colophon.gridBody: 12 columns · 24px gutters · 1280px max · guides shown as permanent hairlines.
colophon.gridHint: Press g for the full grid and baseline.
colophon.sourcesQuote: The grid is “a formal programme to accommodate x unknown items.”
colophon.stackBody: Nuxt 4, Nuxt Content, hand-written CSS custom properties — no utility framework, no colour library. Self-hosted fonts. Statically generated. No analytics, no cookies; localStorage holds the hue and nothing else.
